report_name: ‘Mini Shai-Hulud’ Supply Chain Attack Hits 170+ Open-Source Packages via GitHub Actions

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A threat group specializing in sophisticated software supply chain attacks, responsible for the 'Mini Shai-Hulud' campaign that targeted NPM and PyPI packages.
threat_actors.goals: Widespread credential harvesting, including developer credentials, API keys, cloud secrets, and cryptocurrency wallets, and self-propagation to other software packages.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: The attackers compromised over 170 packages in the NPM and PyPI registries by injecting malicious code into the CI/CD build pipelines of open-source projects.

attack_patterns.name: Hijack Execution Flow
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used cache poisoning within pull requests from forked repositories to inject malicious commands into the trusted CI/CD pipeline of the target project.

attack_patterns.name: Subvert Trust Controls
attack_patterns.description: The attackers managed to have their malicious packages signed with valid SLSA provenance signatures by running their malicious code within the legitimate, trusted build environment, thereby bypassing a key supply chain security control.

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: The primary goal of the malware was to harvest sensitive information from the developer's environment, including developer credentials, API keys (Stripe), cloud secrets (AWS), and cryptocurrency wallets.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Stolen data was exfiltrated using the decentralized Session network, making the command-and-control (C2) infrastructure highly resilient and difficult to track.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The malware used stolen GitHub and npm/PyPI API tokens to commit malicious code to other repositories or publish new malicious versions of packages, enabling it to spread automatically.

attack_patterns.name: Event Triggered Execution
attack_patterns.description: The injected malware executed upon package installation through the use of `postinstall` scripts.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: The attack abused the trusted relationship between open-source projects and their dependencies, allowing malware injected into one project to be consumed by downstream users.

malware.name: Mini Shai-Hulud
malware.description: A credential-stealing malware spread via a supply chain attack on NPM and PyPI packages. It was designed for widespread credential harvesting and used the decentralized Session network for C2 communication and had self-propagation capabilities.

tools.name: GitHub Actions
tools.description: The CI/CD platform was abused by attackers who exploited misconfigured workflows, specifically the `pull_request_target` trigger, and used cache poisoning to inject malicious code into build pipelines.

vulnerabilities.name: Misconfigured GitHub Actions Workflow
vulnerabilities.description: The attack vector exploited misconfigured GitHub Actions workflows, particularly those using the `pull_request_target` trigger, which allowed code from forks to run with access to the base repository's secrets.

campaigns.name: Mini Shai-Hulud
campaigns.description: A large-scale, sophisticated software supply chain attack campaign that compromised over 170 NPM and PyPI packages by abusing GitHub Actions and SLSA signing to inject and spread credential-stealing malware.

identities.name: TanStack
identities.description: A popular open-source project targeted by the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack.

identities.name: Mistral AI
identities.description: An AI project targeted by the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack, indicating a focus on the AI development ecosystem.

identities.name: UiPath
identities.description: A company whose projects were impacted by the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack.

identities.name: NPM
identities.description: The JavaScript package registry (ecosystem) where over 170 packages were compromised.

identities.name: PyPI
identities.description: The Python package registry (ecosystem) where over 170 packages were compromised.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: The code hosting platform whose GitHub Actions CI/CD service was abused as the primary attack vector.

infrastructure.name: Session C2 Network
infrastructure.description: A decentralized network used by the Mini Shai-Hulud malware for resilient and difficult-to-track command-and-control communication and data exfiltration.

indicators.name: Suspicious Modification of Dependency Lockfiles
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Suspicious Modification of Dependency Lockfiles
id: 7c5a8d3e-1b9f-4e8a-9c7d-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of dependency lockfiles (package-lock.json, requirements.txt) by an unusual process. This could indicate tampering as part of a supply chain attack.
references:
  - https://www.analyst1.com/hackers-abuse-github-actions-and-slsa-signing-to-spread-malware-across-open-source-ecosystems/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-14
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\package-lock.json'
      - '\requirements.txt'
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\npm.cmd'
      - '\node.exe'
      - '\pip.exe'
      - '\python.exe'
      - '\git.exe'
      - '\code.exe'
      - '\pycharm64.exe'
      - '\webstorm64.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects modification of dependency lockfiles like package-lock.json or requirements.txt by processes other than expected package managers or IDEs, which could indicate malicious tampering.

indicators.name: Session Network Related DNS Query
indicators.pattern:
title: Session Network Related DNS Query
id: f8b1c2d3-e4a5-4b6c-8d7e-9f0a1b2c3d4e
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries for domains related to the decentralized Session network. This network was used for C2 by the Mini Shai-Hulud malware.
references:
  - https://www.analyst1.com/hackers-abuse-github-actions-and-slsa-signing-to-spread-malware-across-open-source-ecosystems/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-14
tags:
  - attack.t1048
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith:
      - '.loki'
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'oxen.io'
      - 'session-storage'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries for domains associated with the Session network, which was used as a C2 channel in the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign.

indicators.name: Anomalous Script in Package Manager Directories
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Script Creation in Package Manager Directories
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of executable files in node_modules/.bin or Python site-packages directories by processes other than the expected package managers (npm, pip). This could indicate a supply chain attack where a malicious package drops a script.
references:
  - https://www.analyst1.com/hackers-abuse-github-actions-and-slsa-signing-to-spread-malware-across-open-source-ecosystems/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-14
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\node_modules\.bin\'
      - '\site-packages\'
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\npm.cmd'
      - '\pip.exe'
      - '\python.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects the creation of unusual scripts in package manager directories like `node_modules/.bin` or `site-packages`, which could indicate a malicious package installation.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Script in Package Manager Directories
